_comment: Per-profile withdrawal strategy. Merged on top of economicglobal.json — profile keys win on conflict.

withdrawal_sequence._comment_good: Good market: drain TRAD_IRA first to reduce RMD timebomb while assets are up. Roth always last — tax-free growth forever.
withdrawal_sequence._comment_bad: Bad market (conversion OFF): bucket strategy — bonds first, protect depressed equities from forced sale.
withdrawal_sequence._comment_bad_conv: Bad market (conversion ON): convert TRAD->Roth cheaply while assets are depressed. Use brokerage bonds to fund living expenses + conversion tax bill.

readme.purpose: Per-profile withdrawal sequencing strategy and income surplus policy. Merged on top of economicglobal.json — profile values win on any conflict. Only put things here that differ from global defaults.
readme.tax_note: Withdrawal sequence determines WHICH portfolio account is drained but taxes are always computed on the full income stack and debited from brokerage separately. Sequence choice is a tax efficiency decision: draining TRAD first generates more ordinary income (higher bracket usage) but reduces future RMD burden; draining Roth last preserves the most tax-free growth.
readme.income_sources_note: External income (W2, rental, SS, misc from income.json) is processed BEFORE portfolio withdrawals. Net after-tax income reduces the portfolio draw needed each year. When W2 income covers all expenses, portfolio withdrawal is $0 — the simulator does not drain the IRA while you are earning. Surplus income is routed per excess_income_policy.

withdrawal_sequence.purpose: Controls which PORTFOLIO accounts to draw from when the withdrawal target exceeds external income. Earlier entries are drained first.
withdrawal_sequence.note: This sequence applies only to portfolio accounts (BROKERAGE, TRAD_IRA, ROTH_IRA). External income sources (W2, rental, SS, misc) offset the withdrawal target first — the sequence handles the remainder.
withdrawal_sequence.account_tokens: Use account names from allocation_yearly.json. For brokerage sub-buckets: 'BROKERAGE-1:equities' or 'BROKERAGE-1:bonds'. Plain 'TRAD_IRA' means all TRAD_IRA accounts drawn proportionally by balance.
withdrawal_sequence.order_good_market: Portfolio draw order when portfolio is above the bad_market drawdown threshold.
withdrawal_sequence.order_bad_market: Portfolio draw order when portfolio is below the drawdown threshold and Roth conversion is OFF. Typically bonds first to protect depressed equities.
withdrawal_sequence.order_bad_market_with_conversion: Portfolio draw order when portfolio is below threshold and Roth conversion is ON.
withdrawal_sequence.tira_age_gate: Age below which TRAD_IRA withdrawals incur 10% early withdrawal penalty. Default 59.5. Simulator enforces brokerage-only regardless of sequence setting.
withdrawal_sequence.roth_last_resort: true = never draw from Roth unless all other accounts are fully exhausted.

excess_income_policy.purpose: What to do when net income from income.json sources exceeds the withdrawal_schedule target for a given year.

surplus_policy_options.reinvest_in_brokerage: Route surplus to first brokerage account. Best for working years — builds taxable wealth while protecting tax-advantaged accounts.
surplus_policy_options.spend: Surplus is additional spending. No portfolio deposit. Best for retirement when all income should be consumed.

excess_income_policy.income_offset_tax_rate: Approximate effective tax rate used to estimate net take-home for portfolio offset. 0.30 = 30% effective rate. Override per-profile for accuracy: working high-income years may be 0.38+, retirement years with SS-only may be 0.15.
